Precision oncology of SPAtial immune esCape mEchanisms in ovarian cancerSPACE bi-weekly meeting
1Ziqi, AndreasOct 29th, 2025

Meeting Agenda•Go through the experiment plan together & tCycIF-panel•To-dolistfrom previous meeting: •Big update of the clinical information in fall? -> Database has problems to export, follow-up later•Progression: patients with short tracing time should be removed <12 months? -> wait for the update•Find validation datasets •Fit 2 HRP patient subtypes based on spatial phenotype (immune-TSI proximity)•Network analysis (with SPACEstat)•Try analysis T cell network + Myelonets•Change the parameters to test the robustness of the clusters

Future plan

Future plan
•ST experiment(Xenium)•BuildTMA->ROIs selection?

Potential validation datasets•Publicvalidation datasets •With clinical labels to validate the spatial architecture discovery fromSPACEstat:•https://www.nature.com/articles/s41590-024-01943-5; (MERFISH ST data, 130 tumorsfrom 94 patients)

Potential validation datasets•Publicvalidation datasets •With clinical labels to validate the spatial architecture discovery fromSPACEstat:•https://www.nature.com/articles/s41590-024-01943-5; (MERFISH ST data, 130 tumorsfrom 94 patients)•https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-022-05496-1(mpIFdata, n = 100 tissue samples from 35 patients)
Staining for panCK+ CK8-18, CD8, CD68, PD-1, PD-L1, TOX and DAPI

Building-blocks for SPACE discovery1-Overview: Single-cell spatial characterization reveals distinct TME states across HGSC subgroups2-RCNs + SPACEstatsc-level: HR status and progression relate to distinct immune landscapes in the TME3-SPACEstatNetwork analysis: Interactionofxxandmyelonetaskey feature of yy4-WES + bulk RNA-seq: Link spatial diversity with genomic feature and tumor functional state
5-ST: Localizedgene regulatory network-> immune cell states 6-Validations:iPDC&public validation datasetsGenomic change as ground state↓Functional change in cellular programs↓TME architecture reshapes under influences↓Observed: a subtype of HRP patient?

Based on the distance of centroids of 2 hubs•B. Based on the overlapping of hubs with polygons (didn’t consider centroids)We have considered:Combined product of Kernel density mapsInitial attempts
